# Test Fixtures

`tests/fixtures/sbol3/` is a generated cache and is ignored by git.

The SBOL 3 integration tests populate that cache from the pinned
`SynBioDex/SBOLTestSuite` archive when the fixtures are missing. The
checked-in source of truth for expected fixtures is
`tests/sbol3_fixtures_manifest.tsv`.

`SynBioDex/SBOLTestSuite` is the official cross-implementation conformance
corpus published by the SBOL standards body and is the corpus that pySBOL3
and libSBOLj3 both consume. The repository is pinned at a known commit
checked into `crates/sbol/tests/sbol3_fixtures.rs`; the pinned commit is
recorded in `tests/fixtures/sbol3/.sbol-rs-fixture-cache-version` after the
first successful population.

Provenance notes:

- The upstream `SBOLTestSuite` repository does not currently publish a
top-level LICENSE file. The corpus is intended by the SBOL standards body
for testing implementations of the standard. We pin a specific commit for
reproducibility and do not redistribute the corpus through this
repository; the cache directory is populated on demand and is gitignored.
- The earlier `libSBOLj3/output/*` corpus that this project previously
consumed is itself derived from `SBOLTestSuite/SBOL3/*`, so the switch
moves us one step closer to the upstream source.
- Two small local SBOL 3 fixtures (`local/experimental_data.ttl`,
`local/variable_feature.ttl`) are generated by the test harness itself
to cover `sbol:ExperimentalData` and `sbol:VariableFeature`, which the
upstream corpus does not currently exercise directly.
